strait.name: Strait of Hormuz
strait.closure_type: De facto (insurance-driven + active attacks on vessels)
strait.closure_method: Asymmetric denial via drone and missile strikes on commercial vessels, triggering insurance-driven shutdown. IRGC used sea drones, anti-ship missiles, and shore-launched projectiles to strike 19+ vessels since Feb 28. War risk insurance withdrawn or repriced at 4-5x prewar rates, making transit economically unviable. Reports of naval mine deployment began ~March 10; US destroyed 30+ Iranian minelayers in response. IRGC fast attack craft fleet largely degraded per US assessments. Chinese-flagged and shadow fleet vessels continue transiting with de facto Iranian permission. Iran's stockpile estimated at 2,000-6,000 naval mines. Fewer than ~36 deployed in Strait by March 13.
strait.status: Effectively closed to Western-allied shipping. US struck 90+ military targets on Kharg Island March 13-14, sparing oil infrastructure but threatening future strikes if Strait remains blocked. Iran selectively permitting passage for non-Western vessels: Turkish, Indian, and Saudi (India-bound) ships allowed through on case-by-case basis. Shadow fleet and Chinese-flagged vessels account for majority of remaining transits (approximately half of all transits per USNI/Lloyd's List). Approximately 1,000 ships anchored near strait entrance including ~200 oil tankers. Maritime traffic down 97% from pre-war levels. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei declared Strait remains closed as 'tool of pressure.' 2,500 Marines (31st MEU) deploying aboard USS Tripoli for potential escort operations — ETA March 23-28. Three LCS mine countermeasure ships (Canberra, Tulsa, Santa Barbara) operating from Bahrain. No allied nation has committed warships despite Trump pressure (Australia and Japan both refused March 16).

impact.tankers_transiting_current_note: Virtually zero for Western-allied tankers. A small number of shadow fleet, Chinese-flagged, and Iranian-permitted vessels transit daily (~3 per day). Precise count unavailable — most tracking relies on AIS signals which many vessels have disabled.

alternative_routes.name: Habshan-Fujairah (UAE)
alternative_routes.status: Intermittent — Fujairah attacked twice in 3 days (Mar 14 & 16)

impact.shadow_tanker_transit_note: USNI/Lloyd's List reports shadow fleet ships made approximately half of all Strait of Hormuz transits in March 2026. ~3 transits per day, mostly Chinese-flagged and Iranian-permitted vessels.
impact.kharg_island_strike_note: US struck 90+ military targets on Kharg Island. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared. Trump threatened to reconsider if Iran continues blocking strait. Trump later said he may hit Kharg again 'just for fun.'
impact.iran_selective_passage_note: Iran permitting transit for non-Western vessels on case-by-case basis. Turkish, Indian, and Saudi (India-bound) ships allowed through. Chinese-flagged vessels transiting continuously. Iran exporting 1.1-1.5 mbd to China.
impact.us_escort_status: Not yet operational. Pentagon describes strait as 'kill box.' 31st MEU aboard USS Tripoli en route from South China Sea — ETA March 23-28. Three LCS mine countermeasure ships (Canberra, Tulsa, Santa Barbara) operating from Bahrain. Australia and Japan both refused to send warships on March 16 despite Trump pressure. No allied nation has made firm commitment. USS Nimitz deployment extended.
impact.day_15_events: Fujairah oil hub fire from intercepted drone debris. Qatar: 4 ballistic missiles intercepted. Ahmed al-Jaber Air Base: 2 drones struck, 3 Kuwaiti soldiers injured. US struck 90+ Kharg Island military targets. Kharg oil infrastructure deliberately preserved.
impact.day_16_events: 7 ballistic missile salvos at Israel. Continued sporadic fire at reduced intensity. Trump says may hit Kharg again. Urges allies to send warships.
impact.day_17_events: Second Fujairah drone attack — oil loading suspended again. Drone strikes Dubai International Airport fuel tank, flights suspended several hours. 1 killed in Abu Dhabi (Al Bahyah). Kuwait airport radar damaged. Brent surges to $106.18 (+3%). Australia and Japan refuse to send warships. Iran FM: 'We never asked for a ceasefire.' Senate AUMF vote fails 47-53. IDF 91st Division expands ground ops at Khiam, Lebanon.
impact.day_18_events: Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101.00, down 5% from Monday's $106.18 peak, as markets reassess supply disruption severity. S&P 500 recovers to 6,734.87. Ford fire contained after 30+ hours — carrier fully operational. USS Tripoli ARG continues transit through South China Sea, ETA Hormuz area March 23-28. Iran continues selective passage for Chinese-flagged and shadow fleet vessels. Strait remains effectively closed to Western-allied shipping. War cost estimated at ~$21.7B cumulative.
